title: Chocolate crinkle cookies
subtitle: Fudgy cocoa cookies rolled in icing sugar that crack as they bake
excerpt: An oil-based chocolate dough rolled in icing sugar and baked until the surface cracks to reveal dark chocolate beneath a snowy coating. Fudgy in the centre, slightly crisp at the edges.
sourceNotes: Chocolate crinkle cookies are an American baking classic, appearing widely in US holiday baking guides from the mid-twentieth century. The recipe type, a heavily cocoa or chocolate dough chilled overnight, rolled in icing sugar, and baked to produce a crackled surface, is documented across American cookbook tradition. The method here follows the classic American approach with adjustments to British metric measures.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked cookies between layers of baking paper. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily. The chilled dough keeps refrigerated for up to 3 days before baking.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough must be chilled for at least 2 hours and benefits from overnight chilling. Roll in icing sugar immediately before baking, not in advance.

glossaryTerms.definition: The characteristic cracked surface on these cookies is produced by the contrast between the icing sugar coating and the expanding dough underneath. As the cookie spreads and rises in the oven, the set sugar coating cracks to reveal the dark dough beneath. The effect only works if the dough is cold when it goes into the oven.

content.text: depends entirely on cold dough and a generous coating of icing sugar immediately before baking. Dough that is too warm spreads too fast before the surface sets, and the cracks will be minimal. Chill the dough until firm — at least 2 hours, ideally overnight.

Whisk together the caster sugar, oil, eggs, and vanilla until combined. Stir in the melted chocolate. Sift in the cocoa, flour, baking powder, and salt and fold until a sticky dough forms. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until firm enough to handle.

Preheat the oven to 180°C conventional. Line two baking trays with baking paper. Sift the icing sugar into a shallow bowl. Roll the cold dough into balls of about 25 g each. Roll each ball generously in icing sugar until completely coated — be thorough, as thin spots will not crack attractively. Place on the lined trays 5 cm apart. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until the surface has cracked and the edges are set but the centre still looks slightly underdone. The cookies firm up as they cool. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes before transferring to a rack.
